revision.text:
[[ja:穂積阿蘇神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hozumi Aso Shrine (穂積阿蘇神社) |image = File:Hodumori-aso-shrine.jpg |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = 10 Sanwa, Misato Town, Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.621475|N|130.87615833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kenbanryū no Mikoto and the Aso Twelve Deities |sacred_object = |religious_affiliation2 = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = Local Shrine |founded = Tenkei period (938 - 947) |main_hall = Nagare-zukuri |alternative_names = Ono Hozumi Daimyō Shrine
Nagano Daimyōjin |sacred_spots = |festival = October 19 |rituals = |location = {{ill|Misato|qid=Q674749|1=Misato, Kumamoto}} }} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|32.621475|130.876158}}|caption=Hozumi Aso Shrine (穂積阿蘇神社)|width=256}} '''Hozumi Aso Shrine''' (穂積阿蘇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|en|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|qid=Q1043851|lt=Shimomashiki District|1=Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Misato, Kumamoto|en|Misato, Kumamoto|qid=Q674749|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Kumamoto}}. Under the former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == History == During the {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei era|1=Tengyō}} ({{ill|938|en|938|qid=Q24794|lt=938|1=938}} - {{ill|947|en|947|qid=Q24333|lt=947|1=947}}), Lord {{ill|Aso Ietai|ja|阿蘇惟泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso Ietai|1=aso koreyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q76970268}} is said to have enshrined a branch spirit from {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|qid=Q715639|lt=Aso Shrine|1=Aso Shrine}} at Saruyama in Nagano Village, Toyo District, {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}} (present-day Toyo, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|en|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|qid=Q1043851|lt=Shimomashiki District|1=Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Misato, Kumamoto|en|Misato, Kumamoto|qid=Q674749|lt=Misato Town|1=Misato, Kumamoto}}) and designated it as the guardian shrine of Toyo District. It was also known as '''Ono Hozumi Daimyō Shrine''' and '''Nagano Daimyōjin'''{{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568194}} edited ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha, 1982, p. 741). During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1573|en|1573|qid=Q6678|lt=1573|1=1573}} - {{ill|1592|en|1592|qid=Q6769|lt=1592|1=1592}}), the shrine buildings and ancient documents were burned by the troops of {{ill|Konishi Yukinaga|en|Konishi Yukinaga|qid=Q942553|lt=Konishi Yukinaga|1=Konishi Yukinaga}}, leading to its decline. In the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1596|en|1596|qid=Q6785|lt=1596|1=1596}} - {{ill|1615|en|1615|qid=Q6784|lt=1615|1=1615}}), {{ill|Ogata Kunitoku|ja|緒方国徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogata Kunitoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have relocated the shrine to its present site and rebuilt it. The current shrine building was constructed in the 4th year of the {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei era|1=Tenmei}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|qid=Q7752|lt=1784|1=1784}}) and underwent restoration during the Shōwa period''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, 1985, p. 657). == Deity == * {{ill|Kenbanryū no Mikoto|ja|健磐龍命|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|4=fr|5=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|6=zh|7=健磐龍命|8=ko|9=다케이와타쓰노미코토|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenbanryū no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to FR}} with the {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|qid=Q715639|lt=Aso Twelve Deities|1=Aso Shrine}} following him == Festival == * {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|qid=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} == References == == Bibliography == * {{ill|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|en|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|qid=Q1043851|lt=Shimomashiki District|1=Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto}} Educational Committee, ed., ''Shimomashiki District Gazette'' (1922, republished by Meicho Publishing, 1973), pp. 98–100 * By Kyokusui Shimoda, ''Toyo Town Gazette'' {{ill|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|en|Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto|qid=Q1043851|lt=Shimomashiki District|1=Shimomashiki District, Kumamoto}} {{ill|Tomochi, Kumamoto|en|Tomochi, Kumamoto|qid=Q7820089|lt=Toyo Village|1=Tomochi, Kumamoto}}, 1964, pp. 64–66 * Compiled by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Editorial Committee, ''Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Dictionary 43: Kumamoto Prefecture'' {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1987, p. 1496 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|qid=Q715639|lt=Aso Shrine|1=Aso Shrine}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Aso Faith}} {{translated page|ja|穂積阿蘇神社|version=91997854|comment=Imported full ja history}}
